lines.en_lit: and from your they shall give you. No person touch which is prepared for another And one reign does not overlap with another even a hairbreadth.
Hugras ben Levi It was taught When projects his voice in melody, places his thumb into his mouth and places his finger between the strings so that his brethren the priests were abruptly taken aback.

The Sages taught: Ben Kamtzar did not want to teach technique of writing. They said about him that he would take four quills between his fingers, and if there was a word of four letters he could write it simultaneously. They said to him: What did you see that not to teach All the came up a response to matter their ben Kamtzar did not come up a response to matter his With regard to the first it is stated: “The memory of the righteous shall be for a blessing” and about ben Kamtzar and his counterparts it is stated: “But the name of the wicked shall rot”

What But the name of the wicked shall rot? Rabbi Elazar said: decay will spread on their names, that we do not call by their names, Ravina raised an objection an incident involving Doeg ben Yosef, whose father left him a young child to his mother, Each day his mother measured in handbreadths and donated gold the weight he to the Temple. enemy prevailed she slaughtered and ate him.

And with regard to her Jeremiah lamented: “Shall the women eat their fruit, their children in their care [tipuḥim]?” And the Divine Spirit responds and says: “Shall the priest and the prophet be slain in the Temple of the Lord?” See what befell him.

Rabbi Elazar said: A righteous person for his own and a wicked person of his counterpart. A righteous person for his actions, as it is written: “The memory of the righteous shall be for a blessing”; A wicked person of his counterpart, as it is written: “But the name of the wicked shall rot”;

Ravina said to one of the Sages who would organize aggadic before him: From where is this matter that the Sages stated: The memory of the righteous shall be for a blessing, He said to him: As it is written “The memory of the righteous shall be for a blessing.” From where in the Torah do we As it is written: “And God said: Shall I hide from Abraham that which I am doing?” And it is written: “And Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation”

From where is this matter that the Sages said: But the name of the wicked shall rot, He said to him: As it is written “But the name of the wicked shall rot.” From where in the Torah do we As it is written “And he moved his tent as far as Sodom” And it is written: “And the men of Sodom were exceedingly wicked and sinful to God”

Rabbi Elazar said: a righteous person lived between two wicked people and did not learn from their actions; a wicked person lived between two righteous people and did not learn from their actions. A righteous person lived between two wicked people and did not learn from their actions Obadiah, A wicked person who lived between two righteous people and did not learn from their actions is Esau,

Rabbi Elazar said: From the blessing of the righteous you infer a curse for the wicked; and from the curse of the wicked you infer a blessing for the righteous. From the blessing of the righteous you infer a curse for the wicked, as it is written “For I have known him to the end that he will command and it is written thereafter: “The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is very great”

from the curse of the wicked you infer a blessing for the righteous, as it is written: “And the men of Sodom were wicked and sinful to God” “And God said to Abram, after that Lot had separated from him” Rabbi Elazar said: Even for one righteous world is created, as it is stated: “And God saw the light, that it was good” and good means nothing other than righteous, as it is stated: “Say of the righteous that he is good”

Rabbi Elazar said: One who causes himself to forget a matter of his studies causes exile for his children, as it is stated: “Because you have forgotten the Torah of your God, I will also forget your children” Rabbi Abbahu said: one reduces him from his prominence, as it is stated: “Because you have rejected knowledge, I will also reject you; you shall not serve Me”

Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: No righteous person departs from this world until another comparable righteous person is created, as it is stated: “And the sun rises and the sun sets” Before the sun of Eli was extinguished, the sun of Samuel of Ramah shone Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: The Holy One, Blessed be He, saw that righteous people were few, He arose and planted them in each and every generation As it is stated: “For the pillars of the earth are the Lord’s, and He has set the world upon them”

Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Even for one righteous person the world exists, as it is stated: “But the righteous is the foundation of the world” Rabbi Ḥiyya himself said: from here, “He will keep the feet of His pious ones” “pious ones” indicates many Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: is written His pious one”;

Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Once most of a person’s years have passed and he did not sin, he will never sin, as it is stated: “He will keep the feet of His pious ones” the school of Rabbi Sheila they say: Once a sinful act presents itself a first time and a second, and he does not sin, he will never sin, as it is stated: “He will keep the feet of His pious ones”

Reish Lakish said: What is which is written: “If it concerns the scorners, He scorns them, but to the humble He gives grace” comes to impurify, they open comes to purify, they assist him. the school of Rabbi Yishmael it was taught comparable to a man who was selling naphtha [neft], balsam,
